evidence_excerpt: Follow-up brain MRI showed worsening of the affected areas ('mild increase in size of multiple nonenhancing FLAIR hyperintense lesions' and 'concern for progression of leptomeningeal disease'); CT also noted slightly increased axillary lymphadenopathy.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Recurrent seizure-like episodes initially and bilateral intention tremor impacting fine tasks; tremor persisted at discharge despite seizures being controlled on levetiracetam.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Underwent right frontal stereotactic brain biopsy; imaging showed expected postsurgical changes with a small focus of hemorrhage at the biopsy bed.
Initiated IVIg treatment for presumed inflammatory/infectious encephalitis after biopsy showed lymphocytic encephalitis.
Follow-up brain MRI showed worsening of lesions; 'mild increase in size of multiple nonenhancing FLAIR hyperintense lesions' and 'concern for progression of leptomeningeal disease.'
Received a second course of IVIg after MRI showed worsening.
Ibrutinib was held during evaluation and then restarted after IVIg treatment.
Seizure symptoms controlled on levetiracetam; continuous EEGs showed no electrographic seizures despite pushbutton activations.
CT showed axillary lymphadenopathy slightly increased in size compared with prior imaging, consistent with ongoing CLL activity.

notes_on_uncertainty: Available documentation primarily covers the index admission and immediate follow-up; no data beyond the hospitalization are provided. Long-term quality of life and outcomes are inferred from discharge status and imaging trends during this short-term period.
